page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c4m1.large is a burstable virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.t6 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance provides 2 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, indicating that hyperthreading is disabled. It features a highly constrained memory allocation of 0.5 GB, which equates to 0.25 GB of RAM per core.

This instance utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model, which is designed to handle baseline workloads with the ability to burst to higher CPU performance when demand spikes. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps. The KVM hypervisor manages virtualization for this instance class.

Due to the minimal memory capacity and burstable CPU resources, this instance is highly cost-efficient for lightweight, non-memory-intensive tasks. It is best suited for low-traffic web servers, microservices, development and testing environments, or simple automation scripts that do not require sustained high CPU utilization or significant memory footprints.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c4m1.large is a burstable, entry-level virtual machine instance within the ecs.t6 family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at 2.5 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 2 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with a single thread per core. It is equipped with a minimal memory capacity of 0.5 GB, providing 0.25 GB of RAM per core, and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Virtualized via KVM, the instance operates on a burstable CPU allocation model with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. This resource profile makes it a highly cost-effective option for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic automation scripts, microservices, low-traffic web serving, and development or testing environments that do not require sustained compute performance or substantial memory overhead.

tagline: A highly economical, burstable virtual instance designed for lightweight automation, development environments, and low-resource microservices.

Features 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores with hyperthreading disabled
